commands.review_summary:
Adds an opencode-go provider-local stream wrapper and tests that inject an abort signal and abort after post-progress stream idleness.

PR surface: Source +322, Tests +302. Total +624 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established. The issue logs and current source make the stuck-after-progress path plausible, and the PR adds deterministic boundary tests, but no real opencode-go run was exercised.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Remaining action is contributor or maintainer real-behavior proof and risk acceptance, not a narrow ClawSweeper code repair.

target.title: [Bug]: opencode-go provider streaming — API calls complete on provider side but gateway never receives stream termination signal
target.body:
## Summary

opencode-go routes through the shared OpenAI-compatible completions provider. When the provider emits tokens but the upstream SSE socket then stalls (never sends `[DONE]`, never closes the stream), the gateway sits on `model_call:stream_progress` until `stuckSessionAbortMs` (~622s) aborts it, surfacing as `LLM request failed.` / `Request was aborted`. Issue #93610 reports ~90% of opencode-go cron jobs failing this way intermittently.

This PR adds a **provider-owned, abortable** stream wrapper at the raw opencode-go SSE boundary — the direction requested in the linked PR threads (#93640 / #93655 review comments). It does not touch the shared `openai-completions.ts` provider or any shared stream consumer.

### Behavior

- **Provider-scoped.** Only activates when `model.provider === "opencode-go"`. Other providers pass through unchanged.
- **Abortable at the raw SSE boundary.** The wrapper injects a fresh `AbortController` into the underlying OpenAI SDK request via `options.signal` (which `openai-completions.ts` already forwards as `requestOptions.signal`). When the wrapper detects a stall, it calls `controller.abort()`, which genuinely interrupts the underlying fetch/stream — not just `iterator.return()`.
- **Idle-based termination.** A configurable idle window (default 120s — matches the runtime's shared `DEFAULT_LLM_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS`, far below the 622s stuck-session recovery, far above normal delayed usage-only chunk gaps) arms only after the first forwarded upstream event, then refreshes on every subsequent forward-progress event (text/tool/thinking delta, usage-only chunk). A natural `done`/`error` event cancels it. The pre-progress window (request → first event) is left to the runtime's own first-event watchdog, which cron runs deliberately leave uncapped (`resolveLlmIdleTimeoutMs` returns 0 when `trigger === "cron"`). So:
  - **Genuinely stalled stream** → aborted at ~120s with a downstream `{ type: "error", reason: "aborted" }` event, well before the 622s recovery would fire.
  - **Normal delayed / usage-only completion** → preserved unchanged. As long as chunks keep arriving (even slowly) the timer keeps refreshing; the final `done` event cancels it.

### Why provider-scoped, not in the shared runtime

A stall at the OpenAI SSE layer can have provider-specific causes (opencode-go's DeepSeek/Kimi/GLM routing through Zen vs Go catalogs has its own SSE quirks). Fixing this in the shared runtime would impose a new idle budget on every OpenAI-compatible provider, including ones with legitimately long thinking windows. Keeping the wrapper inside `extensions/opencode-go/` lets opencode-go tune its own threshold without coupling to the shared runtime contract. The default 120s is chosen to exactly match the runtime's shared `DEFAULT_LLM_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS`, so non-cron interactive opencode-go runs see no behavior change versus the existing watchdog; cron runs — for which the runtime disables its idle watchdog entirely (`resolveLlmIdleTimeoutMs` returns 0 when `trigger === "cron"` and no explicit timeout is set) — finally get provider-owned termination well before the ~622s stuck-session recovery kicks in.

### Root cause chain

1. opencode-go plugin's `wrapStreamFn` (in `extensions/opencode-go/stream.ts`) previously only wrapped payload/reasoning mutation helpers. It had no raw SSE consumer.
2. The actual stream came from the shared `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts:337`, where `for await (const chunk of openaiStream)` blocks indefinitely when the provider's SSE socket emits tokens and then never closes.
3. `AssistantMessageEventStream` (`packages/llm-core/src/utils/event-stream.ts`) only exposes `push`/`end`/`result`. It has no `abort`; it relies on the underlying iterator finishing naturally.
4. The only abortable surface in this chain is `options.signal` (an `AbortSignal`), which `openai-completions.ts:168` forwards to the OpenAI SDK `requestOptions.signal`. Today only the outer stuck-session recovery (622s) triggers it.
5. Therefore the provider-owned fix has to live in `extensions/opencode-go/stream.ts`'s `wrapStreamFn`, where it can both inject an `AbortController` into `options.signal` before calling the underlying stream and monitor the wrapped stream's event cadence to abort when progress stalls.

## Linked context

- Issue: #93610 (`[Bug]: opencode-go provider streaming — API calls complete on provider side but gateway never receives stream termination signal`).
- Related PRs closed in favor of this direction: #93640, #93655, #93708 (the shared-runtime/compat-shim approach was rejected; maintainer asked for a provider-scoped and abortable fix at the raw opencode-go SSE boundary).
- Provider-owned hook used: `wrapStreamFn` (`extensions/opencode-go/index.ts`).
- Shared files intentionally untouched: `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts`, `packages/llm-core/src/utils/event-stream.ts`, `src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.ts`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- behavior or issue addressed: opencode-go cron jobs hang for ~622s on `model_call:stream_progress` after the provider has emitted tokens but never closes the SSE stream. The gateway then aborts via `stuckSessionAbortMs` and surfaces `LLM request failed.` / `Request was aborted`. Issue #93610 reports ~90% of opencode-go cron jobs failing intermittently this way.
- real environment tested: in-repo vitest harness for the `extension-misc` shard (the same shard CI runs). A live opencode-go/DeepSeek run would require an `OPENCODE_API_KEY` and would not be deterministic for a stalled SSE (issue #93610 reports it as intermittent, ~90% on the reporter's cron jobs but not 100%). The unit suite at the `wrapStreamFn` boundary is the deterministic way to prove both the stall-then-abort path and the normal delayed completion path; production opencode-go streams go through this same wrapper.
- exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-misc.config.ts extensions/opencode-go/` (Exit code: 0)
- evidence after fix: terminal capture below — 19 tests in 5 files pass, including the three boundary tests in `stream-termination.test.ts`.

```text
RUN  v4.1.8 [redacted-local-path]

 Test Files  5 passed (5)
      Tests  19 passed (19)
   Start at  16:07:27
   Duration  2.71s
```

Boundary tests (deterministic, fake-timer driven):

1. Stalled stream is aborted — a fake base stream emits `start` + one `text_delta` (proving the provider produced tokens) then nothing; with `idleTimeoutMs=5000`, advancing fake timers past 5s triggers `controller.abort()` on the injected `AbortSignal` (verified via `signal.addEventListener("abort", ...)`) and the downstream consumer receives a terminal `{ type: "error", reason: "aborted" }` event.
2. Slow time-to-first-byte is not mis-aborted — a fake base stream emits nothing; advancing fake timers past 60s (well beyond the 5s idle timeout) shows `abort()` was NOT called and the downstream consumer received zero events. The idle timer is armed only after the first forwarded upstream event, so the pre-progress window stays governed by the runtime's own first-event watchdog (which cron runs deliberately leave uncapped via `resolveLlmIdleTimeoutMs`).
3. Normal delayed completion is not truncated — a fake base stream emits `start` + `text_delta` + (2s gap, below the 5s idle timeout) + `done`; advancing fake timers past 10s after `done` shows `abort()` was NOT called and the `done` event is forwarded to downstream unchanged.

- Observed result after fix: stalled path — AbortSignal fired, downstream received `{ type: "error", reason: "aborted" }` instead of hanging until 622s stuck-session recovery; normal delayed path — no abort, full `done` event forwarded.
- what was not tested: a real opencode-go/DeepSeek request requires an `OPENCODE_API_KEY` and the intermittent stall itself is non-deterministic; the live provider path is not exercised here. Local `tsgo` reports one pre-existing error in `extensions/opencode-go/provider-catalog.ts` (`Cannot find module 'openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-catalog-live-runtime'`) that is present on `main` as well — local-dist-missing artifact, resolved by CI's build step.
- Fix classification: root cause fix — provider-owned abortable SSE boundary, not a compat shim.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: high.
- Root cause: opencode-go's `wrapStreamFn` never owned the raw SSE boundary, so the shared `openai-completions.ts` `for await (chunk of openaiStream)` loop blocked indefinitely whenever the upstream provider emitted tokens and then never closed the stream; the only abortable surface is `options.signal` flowing into the OpenAI SDK `requestOptions.signal`, and only the 622s stuck-session recovery could trigger it. The upstream invariant ("provider streams terminate") is violated by opencode-go's DeepSeek/Kimi/GLM SSE quirks, so the fix has to live at the opencode-go provider boundary, where it can both inject an AbortController and observe event cadence to abort when forward progress stalls.
- Why this is root-cause fix: because the wrapper is the only layer that can both (a) inject an AbortController into `options.signal` before calling the underlying stream (interrupting the real OpenAI SDK fetch, not just `iterator.return()`) and (b) monitor the wrapped stream's event cadence to detect the actual stall condition (no forward progress between events). Putting the abort anywhere upstream (shared runtime) would impose the idle budget on every OpenAI-compatible provider; putting it downstream (event-stream consumer) cannot interrupt the OpenAI SDK fetch at all. The provider boundary is the only correct owner of this contract.
- What did NOT change: shared runtime files unchanged (no unrelated changes to `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts`, `packages/llm-core/src/utils/event-stream.ts`, `src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.ts`); other providers' stream behavior unchanged (early return when `model.provider !== "opencode-go"`); no new dependency on the shared runtime; no schema/config/migration/API changes.

## Tests and validation

- Extension-specific test: new file `extensions/opencode-go/stream-termination.test.ts` (vitest, runs in the `extension-misc` shard alongside the existing opencode-go suite).
- The wrapper is also wired through `createOpencodeGoWrapper` in `extensions/opencode-go/stream.ts`, and the full 19-test opencode-go extension suite passes with the new layer in place — no regressions in the existing Kimi reasoning sanitizer, DeepSeek V4 thinking, onboarding, or media-understanding tests.
- The wrapper does not change `openai-completions.ts` or any other shared runtime file.

## Risk checklist

- [x] **Provider-scoped only.** Non-opencode-go providers are not affected (early return at the top of the wrapped streamFn).
- [x] **No truncation of normal completion.** Idle timer refreshes on every event (including delayed usage-only chunks) and is canceled on natural `done`/`error`.
- [x] **No new coupling to the shared runtime.** Wrapper lives entirely in `extensions/opencode-go/`.
- [x] **Threshold tuning.** Default 120s — matches the runtime's shared `DEFAULT_LLM_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS`, so non-cron interactive opencode-go runs see no behavior change versus the existing watchdog, while cron runs (for which the runtime disables its idle watchdog entirely) finally get provider-owned termination well before the 622s stuck-session recovery. Can be tuned later via the `idleTimeoutMs` parameter without touching the shared runtime.
- [x] **No shared file changes.** `openai-completions.ts`, `event-stream.ts`, and `provider-stream-shared.ts` are intentionally untouched.
- [ ] **Live provider path not exercised.** See "What was not tested" above.
- [x] **Default 120s matches runtime `DEFAULT_LLM_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS`.** Pre-submit Codex review on the prior 30s default flagged a regression risk for slow time-to-first-byte reasoning completions; the default now matches the runtime's shared `DEFAULT_LLM_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS` exactly so non-cron interactive runs see no behavior change. The default is still far below the ~622s stuck-session recovery that cron runs hit today.

## Current review state

- Local: 19/19 vitest tests pass; tsgo clean for the new file (one pre-existing error in an unrelated module on `main`).
- Idle-timer lifecycle fix: the timer is armed only after the first forwarded upstream event (regression test added), addressing the ClawSweeper P1 finding that arming earlier would mis-abort slow time-to-first-byte requests that cron runs deliberately leave uncapped.
- Maintainer-directed direction: provider-scoped and abortable at the raw opencode-go SSE boundary.

Refs #93610
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
